questions.q: Aurangzeb was a ruler of which empire?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was the sixth Mughal emperor of India.

questions.q: In which year did Aurangzeb begin his reign as Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb ascended the Mughal throne in 1658 after a war of succession.

questions.q: Aurangzeb was which numbered Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was the sixth Mughal emperor, succeeding Shah Jahan.

questions.q: Aurangzeb was the son of which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was a son of Emperor Shah Jahan, builder of the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: In which year did Aurangzeb's reign end with his death?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb reigned from 1658 to 1707, dying in the latter year.

questions.q: Under Aurangzeb the Mughal Empire reached its greatest what?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb extended the empire to its greatest territorial extent.

questions.q: Aurangzeb spent much of his later reign campaigning in which region of India?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb's long later campaigns were concentrated in the Deccan in southern India.

questions.q: Aurangzeb is often described as the last of the great Mughal what?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb is commonly called the last great Mughal emperor, after whom the empire declined.

questions.q: Which famous fellow ruler in western India was the Maratha rival of Aurangzeb?
questions.explanation: Shivaji, founder of the Maratha kingdom, was Aurangzeb's great rival in the west and Deccan.

questions.q: Aurangzeb's reign marked the empire's final era of dominance before its:
questions.explanation: After Aurangzeb's long reign, the Mughal Empire entered a period of marked decline.

questions.q: Who was Aurangzeb's chief rival in the war of succession, the eldest brother he defeated and had executed?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb's chief rival was his eldest brother Dara Shikoh, whom he defeated and had executed in 1659.

questions.q: What did Aurangzeb do to his father Shah Jahan after seizing power?
options: Imprisoned him in the Agra Fort
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb confined Shah Jahan in the Agra Fort, where he spent his final years until his death in 1666.

questions.q: Which Deccan kingdom famous for diamonds did Aurangzeb conquer in 1687?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb annexed the Qutb Shahi kingdom of Golconda, famed for its diamonds, in 1687.

questions.q: Which other major Deccan sultanate did Aurangzeb annex in 1686?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb conquered the Adil Shahi sultanate of Bijapur in 1686, a year before Golconda.

questions.q: Which tax on non-Muslim subjects did Aurangzeb reimpose in 1679?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb reimposed the jizya, a tax on non-Muslim subjects that Akbar had earlier abolished.

questions.q: Aurangzeb adopted which imperial regnal title upon his accession?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb took the regnal title Alamgir, meaning 'Conqueror of the World'.

questions.q: Which Maratha king did Aurangzeb's forces capture and execute in 1689?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb's forces captured and executed Sambhaji, Shivaji's son, in 1689.

questions.q: Which Sikh Guru was executed on Aurangzeb's orders in 1675?
questions.explanation: Guru Tegh Bahadur, the ninth Sikh Guru, was executed in Delhi on Aurangzeb's orders in 1675.

questions.q: Which grand mosque in Lahore was built during Aurangzeb's reign?
questions.explanation: The Badshahi Mosque in Lahore was constructed under Aurangzeb, completed around 1673.

questions.q: Compared with earlier Mughals, Aurangzeb was personally noted for a lifestyle that was:
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was known for personal austerity and orthodox piety, in contrast to his predecessors.

questions.q: Which marble mosque did Aurangzeb add inside the Red Fort of Delhi for his private use?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb built the small Moti Masjid, or Pearl Mosque, within the Red Fort at Delhi.

questions.q: Where in present-day Maharashtra is Aurangzeb buried in a simple grave?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was buried in a simple open grave at Khuldabad, near Aurangabad, by his own wish.

questions.q: What was Aurangzeb's birth name before he took his regnal title?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb's given name was Muhi-ud-Din Muhammad; Aurangzeb was a title meaning 'ornament of the throne'.

questions.q: In which year did the decisive Battle of Samugarh take place, sealing Aurangzeb's victory over Dara Shikoh?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb defeated Dara Shikoh at the Battle of Samugarh in 1658, clearing his path to the throne.

questions.q: The tomb of Aurangzeb's wife at Aurangabad, modelled on the Taj Mahal, is known as:
questions.explanation: Bibi Ka Maqbara at Aurangabad, built for Aurangzeb's wife Dilras Banu Begum, echoes the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Which compilation of Islamic law was assembled under Aurangzeb's patronage?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb commissioned the Fatawa-i-Alamgiri, a major digest of Hanafi jurisprudence.

questions.q: Aurangzeb died in 1707 at which town in the Deccan during his prolonged campaigns?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb died at Ahmednagar in the Deccan in 1707 after decades of southern campaigning.

questions.q: Which rebellious agrarian community in the region near Delhi clashed with Aurangzeb's authority in the 1660s-1680s?
questions.explanation: The Jats around Mathura rose in revolt against Mughal authority during Aurangzeb's reign.

questions.q: Which son succeeded Aurangzeb on the Mughal throne in 1707?
questions.explanation: After a succession struggle, Aurangzeb's son Muazzam took the throne as Bahadur Shah I in 1707.

questions.q: Aurangzeb's eldest brother Dara Shikoh was a scholar who translated which Hindu texts into Persian?
questions.explanation: Dara Shikoh, whom Aurangzeb defeated, sponsored a Persian translation of the Upanishads called Sirr-i-Akbar.
